name: Cooper Kupp
headline: Faded slot veteran is a deep-league dart throw at best
body:
Kupp's first Seattle season laid the decline bare: 47 grabs for 593 yards and just two scores across 16 games, a 69.3-point output that ranked WR47. His 15.4% target share and 0.174 targets per route both slid from 18.5% and 0.282 in 2024, and his WOPR cratered from 0.406 to 0.329. The encouraging notes are a 0.094 EPA per play and 57.1% success rate, plus 6.23 yards after the catch per reception, signaling he can still move the chains when he gets looks.

The problem is volume. Now 33 and slotted behind Jaxon Smith-Njigba, Rashid Shaheed and Tory Horton, Kupp profiles as a secondary option whose 12 red-zone targets didn't translate to touchdowns. Projections peg him near 47 catches, 584 yards and three or four scores, echoing 2025. With an ADP around 202, treat him as a late-round flier in deep PPR formats and a waiver watch otherwise.
